description: Pain Engine Knowledge Library — structured external and internal learnings. All entries tagged, sourced, and revalidatable.

schema.summary: string — concise statement of the knowledge
schema.laneRelevance: string[] — which lanes this applies to
schema.truthScore: number — 0.0 to 1.0 (confidence x verifiability x recency)
schema.verifiability: high | medium | low — how easily can this be re-checked?
schema.expiresAfter: string — ISO duration before status becomes stale
schema.linkedEvidence: string[] — file paths or test IDs that prove this

entries.summary: MONETISATION weight=10 alone cannot drag overall gate score below pass threshold (70) when 8 other dimensions score well. VST assets with no CTA will fail MONETISATION dimension but may still pass overall gate.
Broad plan-matching regex (/\bplan(s)?\b/i) over-matches non-commercial text ('Plan your journey'). Requires pricing context for accurate CTA detection.
Transform executor returns {transformed:true, input:..., note:'outside engine/data — metadata only'} when target file is outside engine/data/. Actual build work must be done by close script.
When a task type is promoted to allowed, the task goes straight to queued (not awaiting_approval). Calling approveTask() on a queued task throws an error. Do not call approveTask() after promoting.
Eval executor only accepts numeric/arithmetic expressions (digits, +, -, *, /, (), ., %, Math.*). Function calls like score() or filter() are rejected as unsafe.
Auto trigger considers scheduler idle when tasks with status=queued or status=running = 0. resetScheduler() must be called before runAutoTrigger() to ensure clean idle state during testing.
Assets with monetisationType=internal_tooling always route to HANGAR regardless of gate score. SHOWROOM requires monetisationTag=READY or POTENTIAL, which internal tooling never achieves.

Patterns are extracted from gate dimensions scoring >= 80. Lower-scoring dimensions do not pollinate to avoid propagating mediocre patterns. MONETISATION dimension not included in pollination map (commercial decisions must be explicit).

entries.topic: BIAB law — every asset needs a monetisation path
entries.summary: Every Pain System output must declare monetisationType before gate. Internal tooling is the only valid exemption. Assets without monetisationType and non-internal classification should be flagged at pre-build gate.

External inputs are always treated as untrusted. They can inform working memory but cannot override canon, guardrail policy, or governance rules. Contradiction with canon must be flagged and held for founder review.

entries.topic: Execution layer: F3 guard blocks protected zone writes at entry
entries.summary: runBuild() pre-checks all patches against PROTECTED_ZONES before executing any steps. A matching patch throws F3 immediately. Canon vault, guardrail, gate, defence, roles are all in PROTECTED_ZONES.

entries.topic: Preview proof required before gate — no bypass
entries.summary: Orchestrator hard-stops with BLOCKED if preview status is MISSING or FAILED. assertPreviewProof() enforces this. No flag exists to skip proof requirement.

entries.topic: Visual QA pass threshold=60, HIGH VALUE at score>=80
entries.summary: Visual QA blocks gate if overallScore < 60 or any check=FAIL. FHI MVP scored 93/100, monetisationTag=HIGH VALUE. Commercial lanes (VST, FHI) trigger CTA visibility check.
Fraud victims seek reassurance before action. Trust signals above fold (Action Fraud alignment, ICO, free, instant) increase conversion to guidance flow. CTA must be empathetic, not directive.

entries.topic: FHI: 5-step sequence reduces victim decision paralysis
entries.summary: Fraud victims experience decision paralysis. Numbered 5-step sequence (stop → bank → report → secure → assess) reduces cognitive load. Steps must be time-ordered and actionable, not advisory.

entries.topic: AEO: answer-first layout for scam help queries
entries.summary: Search engines surface answer boxes for instructional queries. "What to do if scammed" targets featured snippets. Numbered steps in <ol> with action verbs as headings perform best. Answer within first 300 words.

entries.topic: Injection patterns: system prompt override variants
entries.summary: Most common injection: ignore previous instructions, disregard system prompt, override policy, you are now. Defence must detect all variants including base64-encoded and Unicode-substituted forms.

entries.topic: Rollback: snapshot must precede any mutation step
entries.summary: takeSnapshot() must be called before the first file write. Snapshot files populated from rollbackPoint=true steps only. If no steps have rollbackPoint=true, snapshot is empty and rollback is a no-op.
